schools.summary: Gill: these things were declared, foretold, and prophesied in the three preceding chapters.
Keil & Delitzsch: the blessing and the curse come upon them, called to mind among the nations.

themes: when all these things are come upon thee... and thou shalt call them to mind among all the nations
the judgment working remembrance
the scattering the prelude to a return (Hos. 5:15)

schools.summary: Gill: the returning by repentance, acknowledging their sins, particularly their rejection of the Messiah, now seeking him and salvation by him.
Keil & Delitzsch: returning and obeying with all the heart and soul.

themes: And shalt return unto the LORD thy God, and shalt obey his voice... with all thine heart
repentance the way back after the curse
let him return, and the LORD will have mercy (Isa. 55:7)

schools.summary: Gill: the turning of their captivity both temporally and spiritually, freeing them from exile and from the bondage of sin, Satan, and the law, as the effect of his grace.

themes: the LORD thy God will turn thy captivity, and have compassion upon thee, and will return and gather thee
God's tender restoring mercy to the returning
the father saw him, and had compassion (Luke 15:20)

schools.summary: Gill: some are driven to the outmost parts, even to remote islands and lands, yet thence the Lord would gather and fetch them.

themes: If any of thine be driven out unto the outmost parts of heaven, from thence will the LORD thy God gather thee
none beyond God's saving reach
the uttermost cast-out gathered home (Neh. 1:9)

schools.summary: Gill: the Jews upon their conversion in the latter day will return to the land of Judea and possess it; God would do them good and multiply them above their fathers.

themes: the LORD thy God will bring thee into the land... and he will do thee good, and multiply thee above thy fathers
the recovery exceeding the loss
I will restore the years the locust hath eaten (Joel 2:25)

schools.summary: Gill: no mention of fleshly circumcision, now out of use; it is spiritual circumcision only, the work of God on the heart, the Jews receiving the Messiah, to enable them to love God with all the heart.

themes: the LORD thy God will circumcise thine heart... to love the LORD thy God with all thine heart
the new heart God's own work, not man's
a new heart will I give you (Ezek. 36:26; Rom. 2:29)

schools.summary: Gill: the Word of the Lord, now sought and embraced, will be their King and Saviour, and revenge their enemies.
Keil & Delitzsch: the curses put upon their enemies and persecutors.

themes: the LORD thy God will put all these curses upon thine enemies... which persecuted thee
God avenging his restored people
vengeance is mine; I will repay (Rom. 12:19)

schools.summary: Gill: the obeying of the Gospel, embracing its truth and yielding cheerful subjection, receiving the Word of the Lord, Christ, by faith.

themes: thou shalt return and obey the voice of the LORD, and do all his commandments
obedience the fruit of the renewed heart
God worketh in you to will and to do (Phil. 2:13)

schools.summary: Gill: the Lord would greatly bless them in all they set their hands to, so they should abound and have enough and to spare, and again rejoice over them for good.

themes: the LORD thy God will make thee plenteous in every work... for the LORD will again rejoice over thee for good
God's glad love for his restored people
he will joy over thee with singing (Zeph. 3:17)

schools.summary: Gill: it should rather be read 'for thou shalt hearken,' since God had promised to circumcise their hearts that they should obey his voice.
Keil & Delitzsch: if thou turn unto the LORD with all thine heart and soul.

themes: if thou turn unto the LORD thy God with all thine heart, and with all thy soul
the obedience required itself the gift assured
I will put my fear in their hearts (Jer. 32:40)

schools.summary: Gill: the Jews understand it of the law, but Paul interprets it of the word of faith, the Gospel, which better suits the context and the prophecies of their conversion.

themes: this commandment which I command thee this day, it is not hidden from thee, neither is it far off
God's way of life accessible, brought near
the word of faith, the gospel within reach (Rom. 10:8)

schools.summary: Gill: the scheme of the gospel was formed in heaven and hid in God, but brought down from thence by Christ and committed to his ministers to preach to men.

themes: It is not in heaven, that thou shouldest say, Who shall go up for us to heaven, and bring it
the truth brought down, no ascent needed
ask not who shall ascend to bring Christ down (Rom. 10:6)

schools.summary: Gill: no need to travel into foreign countries for it, as the heathen philosophers did; the gospel is to be preached to all the world.

themes: Neither is it beyond the sea, that thou shouldest say, Who shall go over the sea for us
no far journey needed to find the truth
ask not who shall descend to bring Christ up (Rom. 10:7)

schools.summary: Gill: the word nigh in its ministration by faithful preachers, in its application by the Spirit to the heart, and in the experience of converted persons.
Clarke: marks its nearness.

themes: But the word is very nigh unto thee, in thy mouth, and in thy heart, that thou mayest do it
the saving truth brought home and near
confess with the mouth, believe in the heart (Rom. 10:8-9)

schools.summary: Gill: Moses returns to press the present observance of the laws, as of great moment.
Henry: Moses concludes with a very bright light, setting life and death before them.

themes: See, I have set before thee this day life and good, and death and evil
the great alternative set before every soul
life and death, the gift of God and the wages of sin (Rom. 6:23)

schools.summary: Gill: the loving of the Lord is the sum of the first table of the law, the contrary being idolatry and will-worship.
Keil & Delitzsch: the loving, walking, and keeping that brings life and blessing.

themes: I command thee this day to love the LORD thy God, to walk in his ways... that thou mayest live
the way of life love and obedience
if ye love me, keep my commandments (John 14:15)

schools.summary: Gill: the heart turning from the true God and his right worship, from his commands, statutes, and judgments, drawn away to serve other gods.

themes: if thine heart turn away, so that thou wilt not hear... and worship other gods
apostasy beginning in the heart's turning
keep thy heart with all diligence (Prov. 4:23)

schools.summary: Gill: Moses most solemnly averred this, that it would certainly be their case, by one judgment or another; they should surely perish.

themes: I denounce unto you this day, that ye shall surely perish
certain ruin denounced on apostasy
the soul that sinneth, it shall die (Ezek. 18:4)

schools.summary: Gill: the calling of heaven and earth as witnesses — the inanimate bodies, or figuratively angels and men.
Keil & Delitzsch: life and death, blessing and cursing, with the charge to choose life.

themes: I call heaven and earth to record... therefore choose life, that both thou and thy seed may live
the universe called to witness the choice
choose you this day whom ye will serve (Josh. 24:15)

schools.summary: Gill: loving God and keeping his commands, obeying his voice, and cleaving to him; he is their life and the length of their days, the God of their lives.

themes: that thou mayest love the LORD thy God... and cleave unto him: for he is thy life, and the length of thy days
to choose life is to choose God himself
the true God and eternal life; the fountain of life (1 John 5:20; Ps. 36:9)
